---
title: "Building a Research Website with Quarto"
subtitle: "A step-by-step guide using a real research template"
---

## Welcome

This tutorial walks you through building a personal research website using Quarto.

You will:
- Download a working template from GitHub
- Run it locally
- Edit content and structure
- Customize styling
- Publish your site online

This tutorial is designed for beginners, but it uses a real workflow that scales to professional research websites.

::: {.callout-note}
This is not just a demo. You will be editing an actual working template used for research communication.
:::

---

## What You’ll Build

By the end, you’ll have a website with:

- A homepage
- Research overview
- Publications
- Projects or models
- CV page

---

## What You Need

- Quarto installed
- RStudio (recommended)
- A GitHub account (for publishing)

---

## How This Tutorial Works

Each chapter builds on the last. Follow in order, and make changes as you go.

::: {.callout-important}
Do not just read this. Open the template and edit alongside the tutorial.
:::
